en_body:
At dawn the sky is dim
Daybreak upon your eyelids
The gaze grows chilled and aching
You still sleep inside a dream.
Your hairline all tangled
Lips parted carelessly
The pillow hides your jade face
Skin white as Poetry.
My heart is in confusion
Who seeing you would not love
Lying sideways through a lifetime
Entangled in spider silk
The spider weaves its curtain
In rows, in ordered ways
My heart is all astir
Like a thousand tangled threads.
Could I only sit in silence
Dreaming all my life away?
Pick up the fallen leaf
For the wind to return to Heaven?

You lie there, jade face dreaming
Golden Chrysanthemums bloom
Yellow flowers—a single Dream
I stand there in astonishment,
The sun will never rise.
(24-07-1980: UH Campus, Houston, Texas, 1980)
note: “Yellow flowers” echoes the Sino-Vietnamese hoàng-hoa, traditionally associated with chrysanthemums and autumnal contemplation.
